title: Foreign aid given for emergencies and longer-term development
subtitle: Committed bilateral [official development assistance (ODA)](#dod:oda) from governments and multilateral organizations, grants from civil society organizations. Aid for emergencies, such as natural disasters and wars, is often called humanitarian aid. This data is expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.
caption:
Foreign aid given for emergencies and longer-term development, DAC countries, 1971 to 2024

Committed bilateral official development assistance (ODA) from governments and multilateral organizations, grants from civil society organizations. Aid for emergencies, such as natural disasters and wars, is often called humanitarian aid. This data is expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.

The stacked area chart tracks committed bilateral official development assistance from governments, multilateral organizations, and civil society groups between 1971 and 2024, with values expressed in constant 2023 US dollars. The visualization separates total aid into two distinct categories: a lower band representing aid for emergencies and an upper band representing aid for longer-term development. In 1971, the total volume of aid sits just above the $50 billion mark, with the emergency component occupying a very thin strip near the horizontal axis. Through the 1970s and 1980s, the total amount fluctuates within a range between $50 billion and $70 billion, showing a distinct spike around 1991 that pushes the total near $100 billion before receding. A sustained upward trajectory begins around the year 2000, where the total rises from approximately $60 billion to over $120 billion by 2010. The period following 2010 shows continued growth with significant volatility, including a sharp rise around 2015 and another peak near 2020. By 2024, the total aid volume reaches its highest point in the series, exceeding $180 billion. The emergency aid band, while remaining smaller than the development band throughout the timeline, shows a visible expansion in width during the 2010s and 2020s, reaching roughly $30 billion by the end of the period. The development aid band constitutes the majority of the total volume across all years, expanding from roughly $50 billion in 1971 to over $150 billion in 2024. The data shows a clear long-term increase in the total volume of committed aid, with the most substantial growth occurring after the turn of the millennium.
